title: Prometheus の 400 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: Most factual claims are accurately supported by official Prometheus documentation or highly reliable community resources.
The article provides relevant examples and troubleshooting steps.
Minor inaccuracies were found regarding the specific `promtool` command for client-side query validation and the explicit mention of a 'caret symbol' for UI error feedback in official documentation.

sources.title: HTTP API - Prometheus
sources.claim: Prometheus 400 Bad Request errors occur when the format of the query or request sent by the client to Prometheus is incorrect. This error is most frequently seen when calling the HTTP API, and is caused by PromQL syntax errors or incorrect specification of query parameters. It indicates a state where Prometheus cannot parse the request and proceed with processing. The `time` parameter of the Prometheus API must be specified in Unix timestamp (seconds) or RFC3339 format. `/api/v1/query` (instant query) and `/api/v1/query_range` (range query) require different parameters. URL encoding is necessary for user input in query parameters.

sources.title: HTTP API - Prometheus
sources.claim: The `time` parameter of the Prometheus API must be specified in Unix timestamp (seconds) or RFC3339 format. `/api/v1/query` (instant query) and `/api/v1/query_range` (range query) require different parameters. URL encoding is necessary for user input in query parameters. Example curl command: `curl -v 'http://localhost:9090/api/v1/query?query=up&time=2024-01-15T10:00:00Z'` is a valid command for an instant query.

sources.title: Brief notes on making Prometheus instant queries with curl
sources.claim: URL encoding is necessary for user input in query parameters, especially with manual string construction in HTTP libraries, to prevent encoding omissions that cause errors. Example curl command: `curl -v 'http://localhost:9090/api/v1/query?query=up&time=2024-01-15T10:00:00Z'` is a valid command for an instant query.

sources.title: Troubleshoot Prometheus data source issues | Grafana documentation
sources.claim: Prometheus 400 Bad Request errors occur when the format of the query or request sent by the client to Prometheus is incorrect. This error is most frequently seen when calling the HTTP API, and is caused by PromQL syntax errors or incorrect specification of query parameters. PromQL のメトリクス名やラベルセレクタで括弧やクォート記号が正しく閉じられていないと、パース時に 400 エラーが発生します。 エラーメッセージ例: `{"status": "error", "errorType": "bad_data", "error": "invalid expression: \"up{job=\"prometheus\"\n at char 21: unexpected character after expression"}` is an example of a PromQL syntax error message.

unsupported_claims: Prometheus 2.40 以降ではクライアント側でクエリを事前に検証できる `promtool` コマンドが提供されています。
エラー箇所を示すキャレット記号（`^`）が表示されるため、修正が容易になります。
improvement_suggestions: Optional improvement: add official or primary source URLs for unsupported or weakly sourced claims.
Optional improvement: Verify the exact `promtool` command for client-side PromQL syntax validation without server execution and update the article accordingly.

Optional improvement: Consider rephrasing the detail about the 'caret symbol' in the Prometheus UI to a more general statement about helpful error feedback, or seek explicit documentation if available.
Optional improvement: Review current official docs and update changed API/spec/pricing details.
Optional improvement: Add official or primary source URLs for unsupported claims.
